５０年前の小学４年生のままなの知識しかないので全てが良く分かってません
実験、交信、昔話しなどを公開するチャンネルです
コメント等で、良きアドバイス等いただけたら嬉しいです
このチャンネルを通じ、勉強していきたいと思います

snippet.title: Where Summits Have No Name - SOTA Adventure
snippet.description:
This SOTA adventure was another epic QRP cw activation from Summit 2275. Summit 2275 is a rare summit to activate. There are 3 activations from this summit, 2 activations from myself and from W6/JL1SDA. Diamond Valley Lake is a massive pristine lake in the heart of southern California. The trip started with a 4 mile mountain bike ride on the dirt road along the edge of Diamond Valley Lake. The final ascent to the top of summit was not an easy task. The incline of the trail combined with the weight of my gear prevented me from riding to the top.  I had to spend the next 45 minutes pushing my mountain bike up the last 1 mile section.  At the top of the summit I setup my 40-6M OCF dipole coupled to my trustworthy KX2 .Also squeezed in 2M CW operations using the  Quansheng UVK5 with the Smiley 1/2 wave whip. It was fantastic activation from a summit with no name. 
Enjoy this one because it is my best production to date. 
73
DUGBO
KD7RT

Chapters
0:00 Diamond Valley Lake Aerial Footage
0:52 Summit 2275 Ascent
2:23 Summit 2275 Aerial Footage
3:53 OCF Dipole Antenna Setup
4:09 HF CW Morse Code Operations
13:54 2 Meter CW Operations
16:03 Final Thoughts on the Activation
17:03 Mountain Bike Descent from the Summit 


🔥 Support My Adventures! 🔥

☕ Buy Me a Jolt: https://buymeacoffee.com/mountaincwops

🐦 Follow Me on X: https://x.com/mountaincwops

Subscribe & Join the Journey! 🏔️🌍 #SOTA #POTA  #qrp  #cw 

Gear 
Elecraft KX2
Antenna: 40-6M OCF Dipole 
RG-174 25'
Bamatech TP-III
7M carbon Fiber Mast
TIKI Spike for Mast
Quansheng UVK5 with NR7Y firmware
Hi-Per-Mite CW filter


There is no way I would have the confidence and or the skill be a CW operator on the air without the fantastic instructors from both CWOPS and LICW. Do not hesitate one minute to join this fabulous clubs to learn morse code.
https://cwops.org 
https://longislandcwclub.org

snippet.title: Nobody Knew This Side of Rajiv Gandhi
snippet.description:
He never wanted to be Prime Minister. He wanted to fly. He had a secret amateur radio call sign — VU2RG. He drove himself to work at full speed through Delhi's roundabouts. And he became India's youngest PM at 40 — not by choice, but by love for his country.
This is the real, documented story of Rajiv Gandhi. In his own words.
📌 In this video:
— The Cambridge bookshelf full of engineering — not politics
— VU2RG — the secret radio call sign nobody knew about
— The phone in every village — C-DOT — and 6 million connections a month
— Three young men in Delhi before the world decided who they'd become
— The morning of October 31, 1984 — and what he said about being ready
🔔 Subscribe for more untold, documented stories of legends — and hit the bell so you never miss one.

snippet.title: Grid Down? Ham Radio Still Works. Here's How to Start.
snippet.description:
When cell towers go down and the internet goes dark, ham radio is often the ONLY communication that still works. No infrastructure required. No subscription needed. Just a license, a radio, and the know-how to use both.

In this video, I break down exactly how to use ham radio for emergency preparedness — from getting licensed to choosing the right gear to plugging into your local emergency communication network.

What you'll learn:
• Why ham radio works when everything else fails
• Technician vs General class license — which one you actually need for emergencies
• The best VHF/UHF handheld radio for emergency use (and why battery type matters)
• How repeaters extend your range across an entire metro area — and where to find them
• What ARES is and why you should join BEFORE a disaster hits
• How HF radio lets you communicate across state lines when local infrastructure is wiped out
• The 3-step starting point: license → radio → community

Equipment mentioned:
• Yaesu FT-60R → https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/yaesu-ft60r
• AA Battery Adapter for FT-60R → https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/ft60r-batterycase
• Baofeng UV-5R → https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/baofeng-uv5r

100-watt HF radio + antenna + lithium battery (for General class operators):
• Yaesu FT-710 Field → https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/yaesu-ft710
• Buddistick Pro → https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/buddistick-pro
• Bioenno BLF-1215A → https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/bioenno-blf1215a

Resources:
• Free Technician license study course → https://hamradiolaunchpad.com/exam
• Find your local ARES group → https://arrl.org/ares
• Find local repeaters → https://repeaterbook.com

Timestamps:
0:35 — Ham radio isn't just for preppers
2:05 — Technician license: what it gets you
3:30 — Best handheld radio for emergency use
4:26 — ARES: your local emergency radio network
5:30 — Repeaters for emergency use
6:39 — HF radio for long-distance emergency communication
7:08 — Your POTA rig is perfect for HF emergency use
7:38 — HF Technician license rant
8:50 — The 3-step starting point
9:30 — Wrap-up

If you found this useful, subscribe. There's more where this came from.

snippet.title: From Personal Challenge to Park Activator: A Conversation with Martin W5WPS on POTA Performers
snippet.description:
There are stories in the amateur radio world that remind you why this hobby endures. Martin, callsign Whiskey 5 Whiskey Papa Sierra (W5WPS), is one of those people. In a recent episode of the POTA Performers YouTube channel, host Stuart sat down with Martin for a conversation touching on family roots in radio, personal resilience, portable operations, and the community that makes Parks on the Air (POTA) something truly special.

A Family Legacy, A Personal Journey

For Martin, amateur radio was never a stranger — his father was a licensed operator, and radio was woven into his early life. After stepping away from the hobby, he returned in 2023 following personal challenges, finding that radio filled a meaningful void. His story is a quiet but powerful reminder that amateur radio can be far more than a technical pursuit — it can be a lifeline.

The POTA Adventure

At the heart of the conversation is a shared enthusiasm for Parks on the Air. For both Martin and Stuart, POTA is about the adventure — visiting remote and beautiful locations they would never otherwise explore. That combination of wilderness and radio, making contacts from a quiet forest or windswept hillside, is something no armchair operating session can replicate.

Antennas and Experimentation

Martin spoke passionately about antenna building, discussing his use of end-fed half-wave antennas and elevated verticals for portable operations. His hands-on approach to experimenting with designs has been key to making successful contacts, especially in poor band conditions. Both operators also shared plans to experiment with hex beams and rotatable dipoles in the future.

The Brotherhood of Ham Radio

Martin does not hesitate to call the amateur radio world a "brotherhood." Both he and Stuart agreed that the contacts logged in a park are just the beginning — the lasting friendships and mutual support that form around those contacts are what give the hobby its true depth.

Advice for New Operators

Martin's advice for those new to POTA is simple: find an experienced Elmer to learn from, but most importantly, just get out and start operating. Real-world experience is irreplaceable. He also stresses that operators should run their stations in whatever way brings them the most enjoyment — because at the end of the day, this is a hobby.

Martin's journey from a radio-filled childhood through personal challenge to enthusiastic POTA activator is exactly the kind of story this community produces. Check out the full interview on the POTA Performers YouTube channel — and if you have been on the fence about your first park activation, let his story be the nudge you needed.

73 de W5WPS — and good DX to all.

snippet.title: Why Ham Radio
snippet.description:
Welcome to the first real episode of Signal From Scratch.

I’m starting from zero and beginning my journey toward earning my ham radio license, starting with the Technician license. I’m not a radio expert, electronics guy, or lifelong amateur radio operator. I’m a complete beginner trying to learn radio, mesh networks, and emergency communications from the ground up.

In this video, I’m talking through why I want to get licensed, what interests me about ham radio, and how amateur radio fits into a bigger world of backup communication, off-grid communications, Meshtastic, GMRS, handheld radios, repeaters, antennas, and practical emergency preparedness.

Most of us rely on cell phones, Wi-Fi, internet, and power every day. But what happens when those systems are down, overloaded, or unavailable?

That’s the question that started this journey.

This channel will follow my progress as I study for the Technician exam, learn basic radio skills, explore Meshtastic and LoRa mesh communication, test beginner-friendly gear, and figure out what actually works for normal people who want to become more capable communicators.

This is not about pretending to be an expert.

It’s about learning in public, making mistakes, asking beginner questions, and building useful communication skills from scratch.

If you’re new to ham radio, amateur radio, Meshtastic, emergency communications, or off-grid comms, come learn with me.

And if you’re experienced, I’d love your help. Drop a comment with one thing every beginner should know before studying for the Technician license.

Welcome to Signal From Scratch.

Radio • Mesh • Emergency Comms

Subscribe and follow along as I learn ham radio, Meshtastic, and practical backup communication from zero.

#HamRadio #Meshtastic #EmergencyCommunications #AmateurRadio #SignalFromScratch

snippet.title: The Best Antenna You Can Buy - N9TAX
snippet.description:
At the Dayton Hamvention Hamfest, I had the chance to sit down with the creator of the famous N9TAX Labs dual band roll-up J-Pole antenna and talk about the design, portability, and popularity of this legendary ham radio antenna.
In this brief interview, we discuss:
✅ Why portable operators love the roll-up Slim Jim design
✅ Dual band 2 meter / 70cm performance concepts
✅ Portable ham radio setups for POTA, SOTA, and emergency communications
✅ Antenna deployment tips for handheld radios and field operations
✅ Hamvention insights and amateur radio community discussion
The N9TAX Labs antenna has become one of the most talked-about portable amateur radio antennas for HT operators looking to improve range and repeater access over stock rubber duck antennas. Its lightweight ladder-line design makes it a favorite for Parks on the Air (POTA), Summits on the Air (SOTA), camping, hiking, emergency communications, and portable field operations.
If you enjoy ham radio interviews, Hamvention coverage, antenna discussions, amateur radio gear, portable operations, Baofeng upgrades, Yaesu radios, Icom, Kenwood, GMRS, repeater communications, and hamfest content, make sure to subscribe for more videos from the world of amateur radio.
Check out the official N9TAX Labs website here: https://n9taxlabs.com/

snippet.title: The Secret Radio Frequency Over The Ocean
snippet.description:
Ever wondered how planes communicate when they're hundreds of miles from land with no radar coverage?
In this video I tune into Brisbane Center's HF radio live from my laptop in Melbourne and capture real Qantas transmissions over the Pacific Ocean.
HF radio has been keeping oceanic aviation safe since the 1940s - and it's still running today.

🎧 Have a listen yourself:
👉 KiwiSDR - kiwisdr.com
Tune to 13306 kHz, USB mode and listen for Brisbane Center

Want to become a licensed professional engineer in the US? Here's the exact path most electrical engineers follow. 👇

⚡ Step 1 — Pass the FE Exam
Become an Engineer in Training. This is your entry point into the professional licensing journey.

⚡ Step 2 — Gain Engineering Experience
Typically, 4 years under a licensed PE. Many states have now decoupled this requirement from PE exam eligibility — but experience is still required for full licensure.

⚡ Step 3 — Pass the PE Power Exam
The final hurdle. Pass this and meet documentation requirements — background check, references, and state board approval — and you become a fully licensed Professional Engineer.

snippet.title: The Radio Signal Your Clock Has Been Listening To For 100 Years.
snippet.description:
Your atomic clock is not atomic. It is listening to a radio station.

Somewhere in Colorado, that station has been broadcasting since 1923. It does not play music. It does not play news. It plays the second. The clocks in your home that you call atomic clocks are radio receivers. They listen, once a day, usually in the middle of the night, for a signal coming from a transmitter you have probably never heard of.

WWV in Fort Collins, Colorado. JJY in Fukushima and western Japan. MSF on the Cumbria coast in England. DCF77 outside Frankfurt in Germany. Until 2011, HBG in Switzerland.

Five real radio stations that broadcast the official time. Five real transmitter sites you can find on a map. Some nearly shut down. One of them did. The signal you cannot hear is keeping the clocks in your house honest.

Sources: NIST (WWV/WWVB/WWVH), NICT (JJY), NPL (MSF), PTB (DCF77), METAS (HBG).

snippet.title: U.S. Treasury extends Russian oil sanctions waiver to aid energy-vulnerable countries
snippet.description:
美 "에너지 취약국들에 해상 러시아산 원유 30일간 거래 허가"

The U.S. Treasury Department has issued another 30-day general license allowing "energy-vulnerable" nations access to Russian oil and petroleum products currently stranded on tankers, after the previous waiver expired on Saturday. 
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the extension would provide additional flexibility, adding that Washington would work with affected countries to issue specific licenses as needed and help stabilize the crude oil market.
The move would also help reroute existing oil supplies toward nations most in need, and enable countries to compete with China for the previously sanctioned oil.

snippet.title: Nevada DMV Technician Charged After Selling Driver's Licenses for $3,000 Each
snippet.description:
On May 5, 2011, Nancy Brown — a 28-year-old Nevada DMV front-line clerk stationed at the East Sahara Avenue office in Las Vegas — was arrested by Homeland Security Investigations and charged with one count of federal program bribery under 18 U.S.C. § 666 after a joint investigation revealed she had been selling authentic, system-generated Nevada driver's licenses to undocumented individuals for $1,500 to $3,000 per transaction. Brown, who had been employed by the Nevada DMV since 2007, operated the scheme from approximately February 2010 through April 30, 2011, using third-party recruiters to funnel customers directly to her application window, where she processed them without requiring any identifying documentation. HSI investigators installed a surveillance camera directly above Brown's workstation in February 2011, capturing ten separate fraudulent transactions on federal recording equipment; cross-referencing that footage against Nevada DMV database records confirmed that Brown's own user credentials had been used to issue 214 licenses whose required supporting documentation did not exist in the system. Brown pleaded guilty on December 6, 2011, before U.S. District Judge Philip M. Pro, with sentencing scheduled for March 6, 2012, and a statutory maximum of ten years and a $250,000 fine. The 214 fraudulently issued licenses — valid federally recognized identity documents accepted at airports, banks, and government agencies nationwide — were canceled by the DMV after the arrest, but had already circulated for over a year; one cooperating witness told investigators she paid $3,000 to a recruiter, walked to Brown's window without producing a single document, and received her Nevada driver's license in the mail two weeks later.

- - -
Disclaimer: This video is a dramatization based on real events. Some visual content was created with artificial intelligence assistance. Some details have been fictionalized and all names have been changed to protect the privacy of those involved.

snippet.title: Завтра взлетим с заводом! Капотня, очевидцы прилёта. Как это было 16 мая в Москве
snippet.description:
Массовая атака БПЛА на Москву и Подмосковье. Три человека погибли

В Москве в результате попадания беспилотников ранены двенадцать человек. Был атакован Московский нефтезавод в Капотне, пострадала смена строителей, об этом сообщил мэр Москвы Сергей Собянин.

В Химках дрон попал в частный дом. Погибла женщина, еще один человек находится под завалами, сообщил губернатор Подмосковья Андрей Воробьев. В деревне Погорелки в Мытищах погибли женщина и мужчина. Беспилотник попал в строящийся дом. 

Беспилотник ударил по многоквартирному жилому дому в микрорайоне Путилково в Красногорске, повреждены несколько квартир. Пострадавших нет. В Истре повреждены семь домов, пострадали трое мужчин и одна женщина. 

В деревне Субботино загорелся частный дом в результате атаки БПЛА.

snippet.title: Where Did All the Ham Radio Operators Go? The Truth Is Sad
snippet.description:
At the turn of the twentieth century, a new breed of hobbyist started building crude wireless transmitters in basements and garages across America. Professional telegraphers called them "hams" as an insult. They took the name anyway. Within a few decades, these operators had built one of the largest technical communities in the country, surviving two federal transmission bans, wartime equipment confiscation, and an exile to frequencies the government considered worthless.

But something started shifting in the decades after the golden age. The clubs stayed open. The frequencies stayed allocated. The emergency networks kept running. And yet, slowly, the people behind all of it started disappearing. What the FCC's own data reveals about who's left is difficult to hear.

This is the full story of the operators who built amateur radio, what they survived, and what might finally outlast them.

snippet.title: Why VARA HF Is Replacing 300 Baud Packet for APRS
snippet.description:
Discover how Stephen H. Smith is redefining HF APRS with VARA HF and the new VARA Track system. In this in-depth presentation and interview, Stephen explains why VARA over HF is dramatically outperforming traditional 300 baud AX.25 packet radio for APRS messaging, position reporting, and long-distance mobile tracking.
If you’ve ever struggled with weak-signal HF packet performance, missed APRS beacons, or unreliable 300 baud decoding during poor band conditions, this video shows why VARA HF is becoming the future of HF APRS networking. Stephen shares real-world testing from thousands of miles of mobile operation, comparing classic HF packet APRS against the VARA high-performance software modem, demonstrating major improvements in reliability, throughput, forward error correction, and weak-signal decoding. (zeroretries.org)
Topics covered include:
 • APRS over VARA HF
 • VARA Track setup and operation
 • HF APRS mobile tracking
 • Why VARA outperforms 300 baud packet on HF
 • Forward Error Correction (FEC) advantages
 • Weak signal digital communications
 • APRS iGates and HF gateways
 • HF emergency communications
 • Long-distance APRS beaconing
 • Mobile ham radio data systems
 • HF digital modem technology
 • Real-world APRS-over-VARA testing
 • 30 meter and 40 meter APRS frequencies
 • Using compact Windows “stick PCs” for mobile APRS systems
 • PinPoint APRS and VARA HF configuration
 • Future of APRS digital networking
Stephen also discusses the development of VARAtrack, a compact APRS-over-VARA tracking platform designed for reliable HF APRS operation without the limitations of legacy packet systems. (zeroretries.org)
Whether you’re into APRS, Winlink, EMCOMM, off-grid communications, overlanding, RV mobile radio, or experimental digital modes, this presentation is packed with practical technical insight for amateur radio operators looking to improve HF data performance.
Resources:
 • WA8LMF VARA for APRS
 • WA8LMF VARA Track

snippet.title: Ten Meters for Techs - Guide to Getting on HF for Newer Hams
snippet.description:
At Hamvention 2026, presenter Anthony Luscre, K8ZT, introduces newly licensed Technicians and newer amateur radio operators to the world of HF communication through the 10 meter band.

They explain why 10 meters is one of the best entry points into HF operating for Technicians, covering current propagation conditions, worldwide communication opportunities, and why the current solar cycle makes now the ideal time to get on the band. The presentation also explores how propagation changes during the 11-year sunspot cycle affect 10 meter activity and operating opportunities.

The session includes practical guidance on choosing radios, antennas, power supplies, and accessories for HF operation, with comparisons between CB-style export radios, modern amateur HF transceivers, portable QRP rigs, and used equipment options. They also discuss mobile HF setups, simple wire antennas, dipoles, verticals, portable antennas, and inexpensive ways to get started on the air.

Viewers will also learn the basics of HF operating techniques, tuning, propagation, contesting, FT8, FT4, CW, logging, DXing, and making successful contacts on single sideband. The presentation includes advice on station setup, avoiding common beginner mistakes, finding mentors, and continuing beyond the Technician license into broader HF operating.

snippet.title: From a Beginner, for Beginners: How I Learned CW
snippet.description:
From complete beginner to my first Morse code (CW) park activation in just 10 months — this is exactly how it happened.

When I got my ham radio license, I had never touched HF radio, never sent a single Morse code character, and honestly never even imagined amateur radio would become such a huge part of my life. In this video, I share the full journey:

📻 Starting on VHF/UHF repeaters  
📡 Building my first antennas  
🎙️ Making first HF SSB contacts  
🔑 Learning Morse code from zero  
🌲 Finally activating a park using CW

If you’re thinking about learning Morse code but feel intimidated, too old, too slow, or simply “not the type of person” who can learn CW — this video is for you.

I’m not a lifelong operator or military signal expert. Everything in this video was learned recently, step by step, mistakes included. If I can do it, you genuinely can too.

Gear mentioned in the video:
• Xiegu G90 HF Transceiver  
• Homemade fan dipole antenna  
• Portable CW setup for POTA-style activations

Big inspiration:
• Thomas Witherspoon

Topics covered:
✅ Ham radio for beginners  
✅ Learning Morse code (CW)  
✅ Portable amateur radio  
✅ POTA / park activations  
✅ Xiegu G90  
✅ Building antennas  
✅ HF radio basics  
✅ CW practice methods  
✅ Real beginner radio journey

If you enjoyed the video:
👍 Like  
📬 Subscribe  
💬 Comment where YOU are on your CW journey

73!

#HamRadio #MorseCode #CW #AmateurRadio #PortableRadio #POTA #XieguG90 #LearningCW #HamRadioBeginner #QRP #PortableOps #CWOperator #RadioAmateur #SignalJunkie

snippet.title: How ONE Antenna Covers 160m to 6m
snippet.description:
How can one antenna possibly work from the 160 meter band all the way to 6 meters?

In this video, we break down how multiband antennas actually work — including traps, loading coils, matching systems, harmonics, feed-point design, and antenna tuners.

We’ll also explain:
• why antenna size changes with frequency
• why low-frequency antennas become so large
• how multiband antennas electrically behave differently on different bands
• why dedicated antennas often perform better
• and why many operators still choose multiband antennas anyway

This video also covers SWR, antenna efficiency, tuner placement, and the real-world trade-offs between performance and practicality.

Whether you're using HF wire antennas, verticals, dipoles, or multiband systems, this video will help you better understand what’s really happening electrically across different frequencies.

This is Trevor from Telecom Training Videos.

Please like, subscribe, and turn on notifications for more antenna and RF training videos.

snippet.title: Hammarlund Built the Best Ham Radios — Then Made One Bad Call
snippet.description:
In 1910, a Swedish immigrant named Oscar Hammarlund opened a small manufacturing shop in a Manhattan loft. Within two decades, his variable capacitors and radio kits had become industry standards. By World War Two, nearly ninety percent of all American electronic military equipment relied on Hammarlund components. The SP-600 receiver became the benchmark for military and commercial operations worldwide, and amateur radio operators considered Hammarlund the gold standard.

Then, in the late 1950s, one decision set off a chain of events that no amount of engineering brilliance could reverse. A company that had survived two world wars, built equipment for the Navy and the FAA, and dominated its industry for half a century was gone within fifteen years.

This is the full story of Hammarlund, from a Fulton Street loft to the empty factory in Mars Hill, North Carolina.

snippet.title: 🤯 Трамп ДАВ ЗАДНЮ!  Такого КРОКУ від США не чекали!
snippet.description:
Ведуча Софія Трощук розповіла актуальні новини України та світу.
Міністерство фінансів США ще на місяць продовжило тимчасову ліцензію, яка дозволяє окремим країнам купувати російську нафту, вже завантажену на танкери.
Американський міністр фінансів Скотт Бессент написав, що йдеться про тимчасову 30-денну загальну ліцензію, яка надасть «найбільш вразливим країнам» можливість купувати російську нафту, яка зараз перебуває в морі. Очільник Мінфіну США каже, буцімто цей крок допоможе стабілізувати світові ринки.
Заступник керівника Офісу Президента та бригадний генерал Павло Паліса в ексклюзивному інтерв'ю 24 Каналу повідомив: далекобійні удари України є ефективними і з військової, і з політичної точки зору. Бо це можливість своїми силами перенести війну на територію ворога. 

00:00 — США продовжили нафтову ліцензію для РФ: причини та деталі.
03:14 — Як послаблення санкцій впливає на доходи агресора та світові ціни.
04:39 — Удари СБУ по нафтобазах та ВПК у Московській області.
05:57 — Наслідки атак: мінус 10% російської нафтопереробки та дефіцит бюджету РФ.
07:39 — Ефект далекобійних ударів: війна переноситься на територію ворога.
10:10 — Прогноз розвідки Естонії: складний вибір Путіна та виснаження ресурсів.
14:14 — Дипломатичні суперечки: чому критикують «миротворчість» Ангели Меркель.
20:18 — Позиція ЄС: посилення тиску чи переговори з Кремлем?
23:26 — Нові загрози: ядерні навчання РФ та поїздка Путіна до Китаю.

snippet.title: RETIRED ENGINEER'S HOMEMADE SATELLITE DISH INTERCEPTS WHALE MIGRATION DATA 📡🐋😂
snippet.description: Frank Borrego built the dish in 2016 out of a retired DirecTV antenna and a ham radio amplifier. He pointed it at a defunct weather satellite just to see what he could pull. He got static for two years. Then one morning his receiver started printing numbers. Lat-long coordinates. Thousands of them. Updated every six minutes. Turns out the satellite wasn't defunct. NOAA had quietly repurposed it in 2014 to relay tracking data from tagged humpback whales across the North Pacific. Frank had been downloading classified marine telemetry to a printer in his garage in Tucson. NOAA didn't prosecute. They hired him as a remote relay volunteer. He's logged nine migrations since.

snippet.title: CDL Pre-Trip Inspection Tips | Checking Fluids & Air Compressor 🚛
snippet.description:
CDL Pre-Trip Inspection Tips | Checking Fluids & Air Compressor 🚛

Description:
Learn important CDL pre-trip inspection tips at EZ Wheels Driving School! In this training clip, we go over checking the coolant reservoir, dipstick oil level, power steering reservoir, and inspecting the air compressor for leaks during your CDL pre-trip inspection.

Understanding the proper wording and inspection process is essential for passing your CDL road test and becoming a safer driver on the road.

At EZ Wheels Driving School, we provide hands-on CDL training with experienced instructors to help students build confidence behind the wheel.

Ready to start your CDL journey?
Visit EZ Wheels Driving School

snippet.title: FAA A&P 자격증 - 100% 국내 취득 과정 OPEN! #항공정비사 #FAA #자격증
snippet.description:
FAA A&P 미국항공정비사 자격증 취득 과정

https://usaviation.co.kr/%EA%B5%AD%EB%82%B4-8%EA%B0%9C%EC%9B%94-%EA%B3%BC%EC%A0%95

US Aviation Academy(USAA)는 FAA(미국 연방항공청)로부터 2026년, 미국 이외 국가 중 최초로 한국에 “FAA 미국 항공정비사 전문 교육기관” 인가를 받아 운영하고 있습니다.
​
본 국내 과정은 “100% 국내 수업”을 이수한 후 FAA 시험에 응시할 수 있는 기회를 제공하는 과정입니다.

FAA 인가 기관, U.S Aviation Academy 주관 8개월 과정 최종 이수 후 FAA A&P 자격증 응시 기회 부여
FAA 자격증 취득 시 국내 면장 시험 응시 기회 부여, 해외취업 신청 가능
FAA 인가 기관 U.S Aviation Academy에서 과정 이수 후 일반, 기체, 기관 수료증 발급
U.S Aviation Academy 소속 FAA 교관 직접 강의
FAA A&P 8개월 최단시간 과정
총 수업료 $36,000

💬 더 자세한 진로 상담이 필요하시다면?
📞 전화: 02-2661-1845
💬 카카오톡: [USAA] 검색 (채팅 바로가기)
🌐 홈페이지: https://usaviation.co.kr/

snippet.title: This Is Why Hams Have Unique Call Signs #hamradio #radiolife
snippet.description:
Your callsign is your ham radio identity — a unique set of letters and numbers that identifies you on the air, like a phone number for your radio. Every time you key up, you identify yourself: "This is KD0JYV." Your callsign tells other hams who you are and confirms you're licensed to be on the air. U.S. callsigns follow a predictable pattern — a prefix, a number, and a suffix. Once you know the structure, you can often guess where a ham is located just by listening. Your callsign is your proof of licensure and your ticket to making contacts anywhere in the world. This is one term every new ham needs to understand.
